data.title: Hung Hing Printing Sees 2024 Loss HK$43M >0450.HK
Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0908 GMT - Oil prices rise in early trading amid market concerns over supply disruptions. Brent crude is up 0.8% at $76.46 a barrel, while WTI trades 0.9% higher to $72.45 a barrel. Gains are largely driven by fears over Kazakhstan's oil flows following a drone attack against a major pumping station in southern Russia. Meanwhile, the Group of Seven is reportedly considering tightening the current oil price cap of $60/bbl on Russia in an effort to cut its revenues. Traders are also waiting to see whether OPEC and its allies will stick to their current output plans or delay them once again. "A delay could wipe out the surplus we expect for the market this year, which would leave prices better supported," ING analysts say. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0857 GMT - BAE Systems released a strong set of 2024 results, Berenberg says in a note. The highlight is free cash flow outperformance of around 900 million pounds. That stems from high cash conversion in the Platforms & Services and Air divisions, Berenberg says. The company's 2025 free cash flow guidance is around 700 million pounds below consensus, although the 2024-25 cumulative free cash flow is around 200 million pounds ahead following the 2024 performance. BAE's share price falls 2.1% in early morning trade but Berenberg says: "This morning's results should be taken positively, in our view." (Alistair.Macdonald@wsj.com)

0853 GMT - Retail sales volume of Chinese automobiles is expected to drop by 5% in 2025 compared to the previous year, according to Bernstein analysts in a note. They attribute this to demand being brought forward by China's revamped trade-in policy and local government subsidies. China faces a challenging macroeconomic outlook and weak consumer confidence, the analysts also note, adding that exports of Chinese cars, however, are expected to remain a growth driver. With relatively lean inventory levels, industry wholesale volumes may grow by 1%, reaching around 27.5 million units this year, Bernstein adds. The brokerage maintains a cautious outlook on China's auto sector and names BYD and Li Auto as their most preferred within the sector, with outperform ratings. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0850 GMT - Baidu's search market share could be threatened after Tencent's Weixin began tests to integrate DeepSeek's model, Daiwa analyst John Choi says in a research note. In January, 22% of Baidu's search queries were AI-powered but not monetized, the analyst notes. Although Baidu has followed suit to integrate DeepSeek into its search engine, Choi thinks the high-quality information from companies' and institutions' official accounts on Tencent's Weixin platform could attract more users in the long term, he says. Meanwhile, Baidu's move to make its next-generation AI model Ernie 4.5 open source amid fierce competition could weigh on its operating margin, Choi says. Daiwa lowers Baidu's rating to outperform from buy and cuts its target to HK$100.00 from HK$108.00. Shares last ended 2.05% lower at HK$88.35. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0843 GMT - Shares in easyJet and TUI fall, dragged by leisure-travel group peer Jet2's warning that its profit margin could come under pressure. easyJet shares in London trade down 2.9% at 502.60 pence while shares for German travel company TUI are 4% lower at 6.81 euros. Jet2 shares are down 9.65% at 14.14 pounds. Jet2 said that although bookings are up on-year for April, May and June, current macro-economic conditions, pressure on consumer incomes and customers' preference for booking late risk weighing on its performance. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0839 GMT - Hong Kong shares closed slightly lower, with the Hang Seng Index dropping 0.1% to 22944.24. Banks led declines on the index. Hang Seng Bank fell 3.9% after reporting 2024 earnings results and Industrial and Commercial Bank of China was off 1.6%. Chip stocks led gains, with sentiment supported by strong AI demand. Hua Hong Semiconductor jumped 23% and SMIC gained 8.4%. The Hang Seng Tech Index rose 0.6% to 5672.24. China Mengniu Dairy gained 10% after saying it doesn't expect impairment losses to affect its dividend. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0829 GMT - European stocks open mixed, with Germany's DAX rising from its latest record closing high and other bourses falling, after U.S. President Trump said he was considering tariffs of 25% or more on automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceutical products. The Stoxx Europe 600 is down 0.1%, having closed at a record high on Tuesday. Meanwhile, the DAX climbs 0.3% from a record close. The U.K.'s FTSE 100 and France's CAC 40 drop 0.5% and 0.1%, respectively. Italy's FTSE MIB and Spain's Ibex 35--which rose to multiyear highs this week--are up 0.5% and down 0.1%, respectively. There are now many tariff spinning plates in play, but it remains to be seen which of the floated levies will be implemented, strategists at Deutsche bank say in a note. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0828 GMT - Glencore switching its listing to the U.S. would be a positive catalyst for its shares, Jefferies analysts Christopher LaFemina and Patricia Hove write. The Anglo-Swiss miner said Wednesday that it is reviewing its primary-listing status. Glencore's $1 billion share buyback also helps ease concerns over the slight miss to 2024 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization expectations, they write. Meanwhile, Glencore is not expected to benefit from strong commodity tailwinds until next year, they write. Shares trade down 3% at 342.7 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0824 GMT - MTU Aero Engines raised its outlook after delivering earnings which beat expectations, JPMorgan analysts David H. Perry and Lucy Fitzgerald say in a research note. However, the company's cash generation was weaker than expected, which may temper the share price reaction, they say. Fourth-quarter cash generation was 75 million euros below views due to higher-than-expected recall costs on the GTF engine and supply chain challenges, the analysts say. Consensus for cash generation could be trimmed as a result, though earnings consensus for 2025 could rise by a few percentage points, they note. Shares trade 4.7% lower to 330 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0823 GMT - BAE Systems reported sales growth across all five segments, with the electronic systems and platforms-and-services units performing particularly well, Matt Dorset, equity analyst at Quilter Cheviot, says. The demand for BAE's products and services is evident, with the order backlog reaching a record high of nearly 80 billion pounds--almost three times its annual sales, he says. The British aerospace and defense manufacturer has substantial exposure to European demand, just as the continent, including the U.K., prepares to step up defense spending, Dorset says. "We continue to see structural tailwinds supporting BAE, driven by increased global defense spending," he writes. Shares are up 0.5% at 13.44 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0810 GMT - ASR Nederland didn't provide an update on the almost 30% stake that is owned by Aegon and resulted from ASR's purchase of its peer's Dutch insurance business in 2023 but posted solid results, J.P. Morgan says in a research note after the Dutch insurer's annual print. "We would view any move by Aegon to start reducing its stake, accompanied by additional neutralising buybacks from ASR, as potential positive catalyst--but the timing remains uncertain," they say. The shares should react positively to the solid set of results and lift consensus estimates, analysts write. ASR rises 3.75% to 50.58 euros while Aegon is up 1.2% at 6.48 euros. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0805 GMT - Singapore's grocers and malls are set to be the biggest beneficiaries of generous handouts from the country's budget, say DBS Group Research analysts in a note. A set of support measures was announced to help households and individuals tackle rising cost-of-living pressures. Various vouchers will support retail-related spending, particularly on essentials at suburban retail malls, DBS says. Among other beneficiaries are supermarkets and food-court operators, the bank adds. Sheng Siong Group is best positioned to gain as a pure domestic grocery retailer, DBS says. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: China Southern Airlines Appoints Executive Vice President
data.description:
China Southern Airlines approved the appointment of Zeng Yong Chao as its executive vice president on Tuesday, a same-day bourse filing said.

The board also approved the appointments of Chen Zhe as the chief pilot, and Cai Qi as the chief security officer on the same day.

The appointments approval came as Wu Ying Xiang and Wu Rong Xin ceased to be the executive vice presidents due to work changes.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks End Flat on Looming Tariff Fears; Tech Stocks Slip
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks fell marginally on Wednesday as investor sentiment remained on edge amid news of expanding tariffs by the US to be announced by April 2.

The Hang Seng Index fell slightly to finish the day at 22,944.24. The Hang Seng China Enterprises Index also marginally decreased to end at 8,463.10.

Investors remained cautious as fears of a trade war with the US resurfaced after President Donald Trump said Tuesday he is expected to apply duties of around 25% on the imports of automobiles, semiconductors, and pharmaceuticals, Bloomberg reported Wednesday.

Trump added that the levies will rise substantially over the course of a year, according to the report.

The US government previously announced 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum products which will become effective in March.

In corporate news, shares of multiple tech heavyweights slumped Wednesday, with Meituan falling 3% and Alibaba Group and Baidu each logging a 2% drop, respectively.

data.title: BP Investor Group Demands Shareholder Vote on Scaling Back Climate Targets
data.description:
BP (BP.L) is facing a call from 48 institutional investors to hold a shareholder vote regarding any plans to scale back climate goals, London's Financial Times reported on Wednesday.

The demand follows a recent push by activist investor Elliott Management to sell significant assets, including green energy investments, the report noted.

The investors, including Rathbones Investment Management, Phoenix Group, Robeco, and Royal London Asset Management hold a combined stake of 2.5%, according to the report. Elliott owns an almost 5% shareholding in BP.

BP confirmed receiving the letter from investors when reached by MT Newswires.

data.title: Malaysia Shares Edge Lower Amid Investor Caution — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index closed 0.2% lower at 1580.88. Stock trading was cautious, with a downside bias, pressured by continued selling in rubber-glove shares, TA Securities' Stephen Soo writes in a note. A weak earnings outlook also weighed on investors' sentiment, the analyst adds. Soo says the KLCI could find support at 1550 and face resistance at 1605. Among decliners, Mr. D.I.Y. Group was 6.7% lower and PPB Group lost 5.5%. Meanwhile, Petronas Dagangan was 4.0% higher and RHB Bank added 2.0%. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: India's Drone Destination gains on 13 mln-rupee contract
data.description:
** Drone Destination rises 5% to 119 rupees; set to snap eight-day losing streak

** Co gets contract worth 13.4 million rupees ($154,333) for aerial survey and data processing from GarudaUAV Soft Solutions

** More than 81,500 shares traded, 3x the 30-day avg

** Stock down 42% YTD

($1 = 86.8250 Indian rupees)

data.title: Landai Technology Group Establishes Singapore Subsidiary to Facilitate Production Base in Thailand
data.description:
Landai Technology Group established an indirect subsidiary in Singapore, Tephris Tech, as part of a strategic plan to invest in a production base in Thailand, according to a Wednesday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Tephris Tech, which is fully owned by company subsidiary Zyphra Tech, was registered on Feb. 17, with a capital of $100,000.

This move is part of the company's broader plan to invest up to 200 million yuan in Thailand for land acquisition, construction and other related activities.

Landai Technology Group manufactures passenger car transmission gear and shell parts. Its shares closed more than 5% higher Wednesday.

data.title: REG - Euronext Dublin - Euronext Dublin Market Notice (EURONEXT DUBLIN) [70389]
data.description:
This announcement has been issued through the Companies Announcement ServiceofEuronext Dublin.

To view the announcement in full, please click on the associated attachment

https://newsserviceweb.oslobors.no/message/70389

data.title: Piramal Pharma Receives Six Observations from US FDA After Turbhe, India Unit Inspection
data.description:
Piramal Pharma has received six observations in the Form-483 issued by the US Food and Drug Administration after the inspection of its Turbhe facility in India, according to a Tuesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The US FDA conducted general good manufacturing practices inspection at the Turbhe facility from Feb. 11 to Feb. 17.

"Observations are largely around improvement of procedures and practices and not related to data integrity," the company said, adding it was preparing a detailed response to the observations.

Shares of the company were up 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Bic rises on strong Q4 revenue and faster-than-expected margin gains
data.description:
** Shares in the French pencil manufacturer Bic rise 3%, pares gains after rising as much as 5.9%, after the company reported a strong revenue for Q4 2024 and guided for further growth in 2025

** The company announced a revenue of EUR 517.5 million ($540.06 million)and said it expects 2025 net sales growth of between +4% and +6%

** An analyst contacted by Reuters cited the company's Q4 EBIT margin as the main highlight of their result, higher than expected at 6.5%, one year ahead of their plan

** Up to the previous session's close, shares were up 3.1%​ YTD

($1 = 0.9582 euros)

data.title: Fiverr International's Q4 Adjusted Earnings, Revenue Rise; Provides Q1, FY2025 Guidance
data.description:
Fiverr International reported Q4 adjusted earnings Wednesday of $0.64 per diluted share, up from $0.56 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.68.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was $103.7 million, compared with $91.5 million a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $101.4 million.

For Q1, the company said it expects revenue to be between $103.5 million and $108.5 million. Analysts polled by FactSet expect $102.3 million.

The company expects its 2025 revenue to be $422 million to $438 million. Analysts polled by FactSet expect $418.7 million.

data.title: Aldar Properties JV Unveils Residential Development in UAE
data.description:
Aldar Properties (ADX:ALDAR) and Dubai Holding launched a residential complex, The Wilds, in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.

The real estate developer's project, which features 1,700 homes, will start selling on Feb. 27, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: BP Reviews Options for Lubricants Business Including Sale
data.description:
(Updates to add BP's statement)

BP (BP.L) is considering various options for its lubricants business Castrol, including a potential divestment, Bloomberg News reported Tuesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The unnamed sources said the business could be worth $10 billion, adding that the sale is being considered to appease investors after years of underperformance, with a potential announcement of the plan expected during the British oil and gas company's capital markets day on Feb. 26.

The people added that activist investor Elliott Investment Management identified the business as one of the assets that BP could sell. Deliberations are ongoing and a final decision is yet to be made, the source noted.

BP told MT Newswires it does not comment on market rumors and speculation.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: India's Cello World falls after fire at unit
data.description:
** Cello World falls 1.6% to 604.50 rupees

** Homeware products maker says furnace at its subsidiary's Opal division caught fire, but was quickly contained

** No casualties or injuries reported so far

** Co's unit ascertaining losses in fire

** CELO fell 3.9% in 2024

data.title: Eurozone December current account balance €50.5 billion vs €34.6 billion prior
data.description:
Key points:
  • Prior €34.6 billion

Slight delay in the release by the source. The seasonally adjusted figure is €38.4 billion for the month of December, bringing the cumulative total for the year 2024 to €419 billion. That is a step up relative to the €241 billion total seen in 2023 as trade conditions in particular are normalising after the Russia-Ukraine conflict.

data.title: Capital Southwest Makes Executive Changes
data.description:
Capital Southwest said late Tuesday it has appointed chief financial officer Michael Sarner as president and chief executive officer succeeding Bowen Diehl, effective Feb. 17.

Diehl will remain with the company in an advisory role for at least one year.

Meanwhile, Chris Rehberger will assume the role of chief financial officer, and Tabitha Geiger will assume the role of chief compliance officer, the company said.

data.title: Caledonia Mining Names Successor to Outgoing CFO
data.description:
Caledonia Mining named Ross Jerrard as its new chief financial officer, effective immediately.

Jerrard succeeds Chester Goodburn, who will step down on March 24, according to a Wednesday filing.

Goodburn will remain with the Jersey-based gold producer on a consultancy basis to ensure a smooth handover.

data.title: Indonesia Leaves Interest Rate Unchanged at 5.75% as Expected
data.description:
Bank Indonesia held the seven-day reverse repo rate steady at 5.75%, according to a Wednesday announcement by the country's central bank.

Analysts at Reuters also expected the central bank to keep the rate steady at 5.75%.

The deposit and lending facility rates also remained the same at 5% and 6.5%, respectively.

data.title: India's Apollo Micro Systems rises on contract wins
data.description:
** Shares of Apollo Micro Systems rise 12.4% to 119.2 rupees

** The electronic, engineering co says it has received orders worth 120.7 million rupees ($1.4 million)

** Adds that co also got orders worth 59.7 million rupees from the Ministry of Defence of India

** Stock set to snap a three-day losing streak

** APLL stock has lost 8.2% so far this year

($1 = 86.8270 Indian rupees)

data.title: Monde Nissin Forecasts Higher 2024 Sales, Positive Net Income Despite Impairment
data.description:
Monde Nissin expects its 2024 consolidated sales to grow over 3% year-on-year, with gross margins expanding by more than 350 basis points.

The food company's core net income is projected to jump over 25%, with core net margins expanding by more than 200 basis points, according to a Tuesday Philippine bourse filing.

However, the company expects an impairment charge of between 80 million pounds to 100 million pounds for the Meat Alternative business.

Despite this and expected mark-to-market losses, Monde forecasts a return to positive net income after tax for the full year.

data.title: ACEN's Arm Secures First Customer for Retail Aggregation Program
data.description:
ACEN said its retail electricity arm ACEN RES signed up Philippine Cultural College as its first customer under the government's Retail Aggregation Program.

The deal includes five facilities across Philippine Cultural College's three campuses, with a total power demand of nearly 1 megawatt, according to a Tuesday Philippine bourse filing.

The Retail Aggregation Program allows multiple electricity consumers to combine their energy needs to qualify for direct power sourcing from preferred suppliers, including renewable energy options.

data.title: U.S. Isolationism Against EU Hits Euro — Market Talk
data.description: President Trump's growing isolationism against EU allies has resulted in an emerging trend of euro underperformance, ING analyst Francesco Pesole says in a note. The U.S. and Russia have held discussions for a Ukraine peace deal that exclude both the Ukraine and the EU. "Hints at future Moscow-Washington cooperation can reinforce the notion of isolation for Europe from a defense and economic perspective and contribute to a rotation away from European currencies into the safe-haven dollar and the Japanese yen." There's also plenty of room for U.S. tariff risks to be priced into the euro, he says. The euro rises 0.1% to $1.0440. ING expects it to fall to $1.0400 this week. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Talk Roundup: Latest on U.S. Politics
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering U.S. politics. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0405 ET - President Trump's growing isolationism against EU allies has resulted in an emerging trend of euro underperformance, ING analyst Francesco Pesole says in a note. The U.S. and Russia have held discussions for a Ukraine peace deal that exclude both the Ukraine and the EU. "Hints at future Moscow-Washington cooperation can reinforce the notion of isolation for Europe from a defense and economic perspective and contribute to a rotation away from European currencies into the safe-haven dollar and the Japanese yen." There's also plenty of room for U.S. tariff risks to be priced into the euro, he says. The euro rises 0.1% to $1.0440. ING expects it to fall to $1.0400 this week. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0329 ET - European stocks open mixed, with Germany's DAX rising from its latest record closing high and other bourses falling, after U.S. President Trump said he was considering tariffs of 25% or more on automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceutical products. The Stoxx Europe 600 is down 0.1%, having closed at a record high on Tuesday. Meanwhile, the DAX climbs 0.3% from a record close. The U.K.'s FTSE 100 and France's CAC 40 drop 0.5% and 0.1%, respectively. Italy's FTSE MIB and Spain's Ibex 35--which rose to multiyear highs this week--are up 0.5% and down 0.1%, respectively. There are now many tariff spinning plates in play, but it remains to be seen which of the floated levies will be implemented, strategists at Deutsche bank say in a note. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

1958 ET - Japanese auto stocks are lower after President Trump said he was considering tariffs of 25% or more on automobiles. The auto tariffs could negatively affect sales of Japanese cars or their profitability in the U.S. market. Toyota Motor, for instance, sold 2.3 million cars in the U.S. last year. Of that, about 23% were exported from Japan. The administration has said that action is likely after April 1. Trump said he would allow "a little bit of a chance" to re-shore production, without providing details. Toyota Motor is down 0.8%, Honda Motor is 0.9% lower and Subaru Corp. is down 1.9%. The benchmark Nikkei Stock Average is flat. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

1543 ET - The Make America Healthy Again movement could add to Mondelez International's costs as the company is already struggling with high cocoa prices. The effort, spearheaded by Health and Human Services head Robert F. Kennedy Jr., could require Mondelez to change the ingredients it uses in products like Oreo and Cadbury, Chief Executive Dirk Van de Put said at the 2025 Consumer Analyst Group of New York conference. Van de Put expects new rules would be based on European standards, where Mondelez already distributes a range of food products, so reformulating North American snacks would be "relatively straightforward." There would still be some additional expense, he said, as the sweets maker is already juggling high ingredient prices and cost-wary consumers. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1156 ET - Base metal prices rise, with LME three-month copper up 1% at $9,473 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum up 1% at $2,674 a ton. Copper prices have risen 7.5% year-to-date on resilience in manufacturing activity, consumption and investor sentiment, Citi analysts say in a note. At the same time, President Trump's administration is taking time to consider a planned implementation of reciprocal tariffs, compared to prior market expectations of swiftly enacted universal levies, Citi says. By early April, there should be more concrete details on reciprocal tariffs, likely to take effect in the second quarter, Citi says. Citi lowers its 6-12 month copper forecast to $8,500 a ton from $9,000, and shifts expected quarterly average price weakness later into 2025. The U.S. copper price premium over LME is pricing in around a 10% tariff rate. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

1016 ET - Uncertainty over Ukraine-Russia peace talks means it's too early for the euro to enter an appreciating trend against the dollar, Macquarie strategist Thierry Wizman says in a note. Any breakthrough in talks in Saudi Arabia would boost the euro but this looks unlikely in the short term, he says. "Another risk for the euro in the next few weeks is the prospect of higher deficits that may come out of an incomplete peace Treaty." If the European Commission allows member states to use national escape clauses to circumvent the euro-area's fiscal rules to order to increase defense spending, sovereign spreads may rise and the euro could fall on the higher risk premium. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0753 ET - Base metal prices are mixed, with LME three-month copper flat on $9,380.50 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum down 0.4% at $2,635 a ton. Copper has managed to solidify gains made over the past week, amid tariff concerns from the Trump administration and Indonesia's Freeport-McMoRan port disruption, SP Angel analysts say in a note. Trump's 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports have already raised the premium for U.S. prices compared to the LME, and Trump has mentioned copper as being in his sights. Major copper producer Freeport-McMoRan has also reported they are running at 40% of normal capacity in Indonesia owing to export restrictions, with copper concentrate stockpiles building up, SP Angel adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0639 ET - Bitcoin could attract new buyers over time if regulatory changes improve investor access and volatility falls, Standard Chartered analyst Geoff Kendrick says in a note. U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission data on fourth-quarter bitcoin purchases are "very encouraging" for this view, he says. While hedge funds once again dominated buying, bank buying was also strong. Abu Dhabi's sovereign wealth fund reported a 4,700 bitcoin equivalent position in Blackrock's iShares Bitcoin Trust exchange traded fund. "While this is small for now, we would expect the size to increase over time and, indeed, for other sovereigns to also start buying." StanChart expects bitcoin to reach $500,000 before President Trump leaves office in 2029. Bitcoin falls 0.7% to $95,761, according to LSEG. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0555 ET - The dollar could fall if upcoming data show a further fall in U.S. business sentiment, Commerzbank analyst Volkmar Baur says in a note. Many U.S. companies initially reacted positively to President Trump's November election victory but the picture changed in January, he says. Companies are unsettled by the rapid succession and partial reversal of Trump's announcements, some of which lack key details. A number of sentiment indicators for February will be released in coming days including the New York Fed's Empire Manufacturing Survey at 1330 GMT. "If there are further setbacks, the dollar could face increasing headwinds." That's because the U.S. economic growth advantage is an important factor supporting the dollar. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0506 ET - Only European pressure can bring about peace in Ukraine, France's top diplomat says as talks begin between the U.S. and Russia, without the presence of European or Ukrainian leaders. Europe's political leaders have gathered in Paris in response to their sidelining from peace talks, and are united over the need to ensure lasting peace for Ukraine following its full-scale invasion by neighboring Russia three years ago, Jean-Noël Barrot tells radio station France Info. Any peace agreement must dissuade Russia from any new aggression against its neighbors, the foreign minister says. And it is Europeans who can bring about security guarantees for Kiev, Barrot adds. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0428 ET - European defense stocks continue their steep upward trend in morning trade. U.S. and Russian officials are holding talks in Saudi Arabia on improving bilateral relations and potentially halting the war in Ukraine, and key European leaders discussed security guarantees for Ukraine in the event of a ceasefire deal. Germany's Renk Group was the top European defense performer, rising 8.6% at 31.7 euros, followed by sensors provider Hensoldt, up 4% at 48.16 euros. Shares in Italy's Fincantieri advance 3.7% at 8.76 euros, while those in Britain's QinetiQ increase 2.9% at 4.03 pounds. France's Thales and Germany's Rheinmetall lag behind, rising 1.5% at 180.05 euros and 1.65% at 949 euros respectively. Shares in Italy's Leonardo go up 1.2% at 34.66 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI Index Ends 0.2% Lower at 1580.88
Elliott Statement on Aspen Technology, Inc.
data.description:
Elliott Statement on Aspen Technology, Inc.

PR Newswire

WEST PALM BEACH, Fla., Feb. 19, 2025

WEST PALM BEACH, Fla., Feb. 19, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Elliott Investment Management L.P. ("Elliott"), which manages funds that together have an investment of more than $1.5 billion in Aspen Technology, Inc. (the "Company" or "AspenTech"), today issued the following statement regarding the tender offer (the "Tender Offer") by Emerson Electric Co. ("Emerson") for all AspenTech shares it does not currently own:

"Emerson's proposal to acquire AspenTech is clearly the result of a conflicted and deeply flawed process, bearing all the hallmarks of an opportunistic minority squeeze-out. Furthermore, Emerson's public statements and actions leading up to the commencement of the Tender Offer underscore its coercive intent toward minority stockholders.

This transaction is unfair to minority stockholders, and we intend to pursue all appropriate remedies against Emerson."

About Elliott

Elliott Investment Management L.P. (together with its affiliates, "Elliott") manages approximately $69.7 billion of assets as of June 30, 2024. Founded in 1977, it is one of the oldest funds under continuous management. The Elliott funds' investors include pension plans, sovereign wealth funds, endowments, foundations, funds-of-funds, high net worth individuals and families, and employees of the firm.

Media Contact

Alice Best

Elliott Advisors (UK) Limited

T: +44 203 009 1715

abest@elliottadvisors.co.uk

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/elliott-statement-on-aspen-technology-inc-302379600.html

SOURCE Elliott Investment Management L.P.

data.title: Keystone Realtors to Acquire Land Parcel in Nagpur, India for INR200 Million
data.description:
Keystone Realtors has agreed to acquire a land parcel in Nagpur, India for 200 million rupees from individual sellers Sangita Talmale, Bhavesh Talmale, and Aayush Talmale, according to a Wednesday filing with the Indian stock exchanges.

The 13,473-square meter land is being acquired to participate in the larger land parcel envisaged to be developed.

Shares of the company were up over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Invitation to the presentation of Qben Infra’s year-end report 2024
data.description:
Qben Infra AB (publ) invites you to attend its Q4 2024 interim report presentation. The presentation will take place at 10.00 am CET on Wednesday February 26, 2025.

Øivind Horpestad, CEO of Qben Infra, will present the Q4 report. Following the presentation a Q&A session will be held. The presentation will be held in Swedish.

Time: Wednesday February 26, 10.00 am CET

Join the webcast here (no pre-registration is required). The audience will have the possibility to ask written questions in the chat.

Presentation material and a recording of the webcast will be made available here afterward.

For further information, please contact:Øivind Horpestad, CEO of Qben Infra

Tel: +47 910 00 626

E-mail: oivind@qben.se

About Qben InfraQben Infra is a group specializing in the acquisition and development of platforms within infrastructure services in the Nordics, gathering a diversified portfolio of 20+ companies across four infrastructure niches: Construction, Rail, Power and Testing, Inspection & Certification (TIC). To drive value creation, Qben Infra collaborates closely with the platforms, focusing on identifying investment opportunities within profitable niches with strong potential for development, consolidation, and growth. This strategy fosters sustainable growth and profitability, enabling Qben Infra to play a vital role in advancing infrastructure development in the Nordics, now and going forward. By merging platforms with distinct yet complementary expertise, Qben Infra unlocks synergies and capitalizes on expansive market opportunities, enabling the group to take on even larger and more complex projects. For further information, visit qben.se.

data.title: Devyser secures a major SEK 16.8M tender in Italy with 30% growth
data.description:
Devyser a leading provider of genetic testing solutions, has been awarded a significant tender for the supply of its comprehensive next-generation sequencing (NGS) tests for critical genetic markers. The tender with AOU Città Della Salute e della Scienza, Torino, Italy covers CFTR, BRCA and Thalassemia genetic testing and is valid for a period of three years. Indicative order value is estimated to amount to 16.8 million SEK.

This tender underscores Devyser’s continued excellence in delivering high-quality genetic testing solutions and its commitment to advancing precision medicine. The total contract value amounts to 16.8 million SEK, representing an approximate 30% growth compared to the previous tender. The hospital’s decision to extend the partnership with Devyser was based on the company’s proven track record of compliance and the superior performance of its NGS tests.

The tender strengthens our already strong position in the Italian market, and I am very proud of the entire team who made this happen", says CCO Theis Kipling. "This tender is another confirmation of Devyser’s position in the European healthcare market, reaffirming its role as a trusted partner for advanced genetic diagnostics.” The company remains dedicated to supporting healthcare providers with innovative and reliable solutions that enhance patient care and clinical outcomes.
